skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic brand colors, typography guidance, and visual styling rules to artifacts that need a consistent Anthropic-style presentation.
skill.summary: Apply Anthropic-style colors, typography, and visual hierarchy to design artifacts.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Reviewed 21 static findings across SKILL.md and LICENSE.txt. All flagged patterns are false positives from Apache license text, Markdown code spans for color values, or descriptive brand-guideline prose; no executable code, network calls, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Hardcoded License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links in LICENSE.txt. They are not fetched by the skill and do not create network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are license reference text only. No code path or instruction directs the assistant to open or transmit data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto Matches in License Text
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur inside ordinary Apache License prose. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or encryption operation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows legal boilerplate, not implementation code. The matches appear to be substring detections in words from the license text.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The backticks in SKILL.md wrap hex color values as Markdown inline code. They are not Ruby or shell command substitution and cannot execute.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is a color palette list. Each backticked token is a static hex color string, not executable syntax.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Skill Text Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The remaining SKILL.md alerts occur in descriptive metadata, keyword text, font fallback guidance, a color entry, and an RGBColor reference. No system or network reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found brand-guideline prose only. There are no commands, imports, sockets, subprocess calls, or instructions to gather host or network information.

content.user_title: Apply Brand Guidelines to Visual Assets
content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent brand styling across slides, documents, and generated visuals. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear color, typography, and hierarchy rules for Anthropic-style artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Lists primary, neutral, and accent colors with exact hex values.
Defines heading and body typography choices with fallback fonts.
Explains how to style headings, body text, shapes, and accents.
Guides brand-consistent visual formatting for documents and presentations.
Provides color application notes using RGB values for precise matching.
limitations: Does not include scripts, templates, or automated asset-generation code.
Does not install Poppins, Lora, or any other font files.
Does not verify legal permission to use Anthropic trademarks or brand assets.
Does not include image files, logos, or complete design-system components.

use_cases.title: Style Executive Presentations
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, fonts, and accent treatment to slide decks that need an Anthropic-style visual language.

use_cases.title: Format Internal Documents
use_cases.description: Use the documented palette and typography rules to make reports and handouts visually consistent.

use_cases.title: Guide Design Reviews
use_cases.description: Check whether visual assets follow the listed color, type, and hierarchy guidance before publication.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Brand Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner styling pass for a simple artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand-guidelines skill to this artifact. Update colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent shapes using the documented palette.

prompt_templates.title: Review Brand Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of an existing document or presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact against the brand-guidelines skill. List the styling changes needed for colors, typography, hierarchy, and accents.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Styled Layout Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning before building a visual asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a layout plan using the brand-guidelines skill. Specify heading treatment, body text treatment, background colors, accents, and fallback fonts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Multi-Page Artifact
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced quality review across several pages or slides.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this multi-page artifact with the brand-guidelines skill. Identify inconsistent color use, weak hierarchy, typography issues, and shape styling problems.

output_examples.input: A slide deck uses many unrelated accent colors and default fonts.
output_examples.output: The review recommends the dark and light neutrals for structure, orange for primary accents, and Poppins or Arial for headings.

output_examples.input: A report needs a clear visual hierarchy before publication.
output_examples.output: The guidance separates heading and body text treatment, assigns readable fallback fonts, and suggests restrained accent colors for diagrams.

output_examples.input: A product one-pager needs a brand-consistent polish pass.
output_examples.output: The styling plan defines neutral backgrounds, readable text colors, and a controlled rotation of orange, blue, and green accents.

best_practices: Use the exact hex values when color fidelity matters.
Install Poppins and Lora before final export when brand typography is required.
Keep accent colors secondary to the main content hierarchy.
anti_patterns: Do not use the palette as proof of trademark or brand-use permission.
Do not mix unrelated font families into the same artifact without a clear reason.
Do not apply all accent colors with equal emphasis on one page.

faq.question: Does this skill run code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and provides brand styling guidance.

faq.question: Does it include official font files?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Poppins and Lora, with Arial and Georgia as fallbacks.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides text guidance that Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can use when editing artifacts.

faq.question: Does it provide logos or image assets?
faq.answer: No. It only documents colors, typography, and styling behavior.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable code, network behavior, or prompt injection.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing work?
faq.answer: Users should confirm they have permission to use any protected brand names, trademarks, or assets.
